relevant_files.role: Orchestrates scanning of PR diffs; currently operates line-by-line on added lines.
Regex-based pattern engine; iterates through lines and matches against pre-compiled regexes.
Database of security patterns with regex, severity, and CWE metadata.
Data structures for Finding, PatternDefinition, Severity, and Confidence.
Integration tests documenting the current limitation of multi-line detection.

conventions.commits: Conventional commits (feat, fix, test, etc.) with GPG and DCO signing.
conventions.testing: AAA pattern; unit tests in modules, integration tests in tests/ directory; use of fixtures.
conventions.linting: Strict clippy (pedantic) and rustfmt; unsafe_code warned; unused_must_use denied.
conventions.error_handling: thiserror for libraries (aptu-core), anyhow for applications (aptu-cli).

patterns: Line-by-line scanning of diff hunks in SecurityScanner::scan_diff
Static LazyLock for global PatternEngine initialization

related_issues.title: feat(security): evaluate tree-sitter for AST-based vulnerability detection
related_issues.relevance: Primary issue: target for AST-based scanning evaluation.

related_issues.title: test(security): add negative test documenting multi-line detection limitation
related_issues.relevance: Provides the baseline test case that fails for multi-line vulnerabilities.

related_issues.title: feat(security): initial regex-based security scanner
related_issues.relevance: Context for the current implementation and its known limitations.

constraints: READ-ONLY: No code changes or commits allowed during research.
Performance: Scanning must remain fast (tree-sitter incremental parsing is a benefit).
Binary size: Grammar crates increase binary size.
Language support: Each language requires a specific tree-sitter grammar crate.
test_coverage: High for regex patterns; includes integration tests for SQLi, XSS, and crypto; explicitly documents multi-line failure.

library_findings.notes: Core library for AST parsing and querying.
Grammar for Rust; essential for the initial spike.

approaches.name: Hybrid Regex-AST Scanner
approaches.description: Retain regex for simple single-line patterns (e.g., hardcoded keys) and introduce Tree-sitter for complex multi-line patterns (e.g., SQLi).
approaches.pros: Maintains performance for simple cases; solves multi-line detection; lower migration risk.
approaches.cons: Increased complexity from managing two engines; larger binary size.

approaches.name: Full Tree-sitter Migration
approaches.description: Convert all regex patterns to Tree-sitter queries and use AST parsing for all security scanning.
approaches.pros: Uniform scanning engine; high precision; better handling of language-specific nuances.
approaches.cons: Significant effort to rewrite all patterns; potentially slower for simple patterns; requires grammars for all supported extensions.

recommendation: Implement the Hybrid approach. Start by adding tree-sitter and tree-sitter-rust to aptu-core. Introduce an AstEngine that runs in parallel with PatternEngine. Update SecurityScanner to aggregate findings from both. This allows for incremental adoption and handles the immediate need for multi-line SQLi detection in Rust.
